rationale: In the General Discussion, the citing article explicitly summarizes Zhu and Meyers-Levy's account that music's semantic meaning affects product perceptions depending on processing and resource conditions. It then positions its own results as adding different boundary conditions, namely that music-context congruence matters mainly when consumers are self-focused and the cue matches product-use context. This is more than a passing literature-list mention because the cited paper is used to frame and interpret the citing paper's claimed contribution on background-music effects.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Zhu and Meyers-Levy (2005) as prior theory on how background music can shape product perceptions, then extends that line by introducing self-focused attention and context-product congruence as additional conditions for when music influences evaluations and choices.
